title: Perfect Console Bookshelf
summary: A simple 1x12 shelf-and-leg console with three open shelves and an optional plywood back — a plainer, leg-based build distinct from this catalog's face-frame console tables.
description:
This console is built almost entirely from 1x12 boards on simple leg assemblies, with none of the face-frame or plywood-carcass joinery this catalog's other consoles use — a genuinely simpler, more accessible build for someone without a table saw or pocket hole jig lying around. Build the two leg assemblies first, joining the legs with a top and bottom stretcher on each side, then set the three shelves across them at even spacing.

The shelves double as the console's main structural connection between the two leg assemblies — there's no separate carcass box holding it together, so the shelf-to-leg joints are what keep the whole piece square and rigid. **Attach the shelves square to the legs before adding the optional back panel**, since the back panel doesn't add meaningful structural rigidity here; it's a finish detail that hides the wall behind the open shelves, not a load-bearing part.

This is a genuinely fast build for the size of the finished piece, and its plainness is the point — it's meant to take a bench-top upgrade to any room without asking for tools most casual builders don't already own.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the leg assemblies
steps.body: Attach a top and bottom stretcher between each pair of legs with 2-1/2" screws and glue, forming two identical leg assemblies. Check square with a speed square.

steps.title: Attach the shelves
steps.body: Set the three shelves across the two leg assemblies at even spacing and attach with 2-1/2" screws driven through the legs into the shelf ends, checking the whole assembly stays square.

steps.title: Add the back panel (optional)
steps.body: Cut the plywood back panel to fit and attach with 3/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Simple console table with three open shelves
